scenario.title: The Missing Background
scenario.context: You're a product manager who needs help writing release notes for a new feature. The feature is a 'dark mode' toggle for your mobile app, but the AI has no idea about your product, users, or communication style.
scenario.goal: Write a prompt that provides sufficient background context so the AI can write release notes that sound like they came from your company.
constraints: Include at least 3 pieces of relevant context about your product/company
scenario.exampleInput: Feature: Dark mode toggle in app settings. Technical: Reduces battery usage by 20% on OLED screens.
hints: What does the AI need to know about your product to write appropriately?
Who reads these release notes? Technical users? General public?
What's your brand voice? Casual? Corporate? Playful?

rubric.name: Product Context
rubric.description: Does the prompt provide relevant product/company information?

scoringGuide.excellent: Clear product description, target market, and brand identity

rubric.name: Audience Definition
rubric.description: Is the target audience clearly defined?

rubric.name: Tone Specification
rubric.description: Is the desired voice/tone clearly communicated?

scoringGuide.excellent: Explicit tone description with examples or reference

rubric.name: Context Efficiency
rubric.description: Is the context concise but sufficient?

scoringGuide.poor: Either too sparse or too verbose

evaluatorContext.idealResponse:
Context: FocusApp is a productivity app for remote workers (25-45 age range). Our brand voice is friendly but professional - we avoid corporate jargon and speak directly to users. Our release notes are typically 2-3 sentences, focusing on user benefits over technical details.

Task: Write release notes for our new dark mode feature. The feature saves 20% battery on OLED screens, but lead with the user experience benefit (easier on eyes, better for night use). Match our existing style: conversational, benefit-focused, no version numbers or technical specs.
commonMistakes: Assuming the AI knows your product
Not specifying who reads the release notes
